folio.label: 26r
folio.l: Lust and array , and alle the circumstaunces
Of loue , which æ I rekened , and reken shalle
Be ordre weren peynted , on the walle
And moo than I can make of , menciou~
For sothly , al the Mount of Sytherou~
There venus hath , hire péncipal dwellyng
Was shewed on the wal , in portraiyng
With al the gardeyn , and the lustynesse
Nat was for ©eten , the porter Idelnesse
Ne Narsisus , the faire , of ©ore a gon
Ne ©et the folie , of kyng Salamon
Ne ©et the gret strengthe , of Hercules
The enchauntement , of Medea and Circes
Ne of Turnus , w the hardy fiers corage
The riche Cresus , kaytif in seruage

note.notetxt: seruyage1088s is has a double stem.ODR

folio.l: ¢ Thus may ©e sen , æ wysdom ne richesse
Beaute ne sleigút , strengthe hardynesse
Ne may with venus , maken cham¹tye
For as hire lust , æe world æa~ne may she gye
Loo alle æeise folk , so caugút were in hire laas
Til they for woo , ful often seid allas
¢ Suffisith here , ensamples on or two
And ©et I koude reken , a , M , mo
The statute of venus , glorious for to se
Was naked , fletyng in the large see
And fro the nauyl doun , al couered was
With waughes grene , & brigút as eny glas
A Citole , in hire rigút hand , had she
And on hire hede , ful semely for to se
A Rose garlond , fressú & wel smellyng
A bouen hire hede , hire dowes flekeryng
Biforn~ hire stod , hire sone Cupido
Vp on hise shuldres , wynges had he two
And blynd he was , as it is oft sene
A bowe he bar , and arwes brigút & kene
¢ Why shuld I nat eke , as wel telle ©ow aÂ
The portrature , æ was vp on the waÂ
W Inne the temple , of mygúty Mars the rede
Al peynted was the wal , in lengthe & brede
Lyke to the Estres , of the grisly place
That higút the gret temple , of Mars in Trace
In thilk colde frosty , regiou~

note.notetxt: 1115cross in dry point in the bottom left margin.ODR
